acceptanceCriteria: A new module `apps/web/src/lib/chunk-text.ts` exports a single named function `chunkText(text: string, maxLength: number): string[]`, following repo conventions (JSDoc, no default export, pure function).
Empty string or whitespace-only input returns an empty array `[]`.
Input whose length ≤ `maxLength` returns a single-element array `[text]` with the input unchanged.

When text exceeds `maxLength`, it is split at sentence boundaries (`.`, `!`, `?` followed by whitespace), packing consecutive sentences into a chunk until the next sentence would cause the chunk to exceed `maxLength`.
A single sentence longer than `maxLength` is hard-split into `maxLength`-sized pieces; no characters are dropped.
No returned chunk ever has `.length > maxLength`.
Concatenating all returned chunks reproduces the input's non-whitespace content in order — no content is lost or reordered.
Passing `maxLength <= 0` throws a clear, descriptive error.

A colocated Vitest suite (`apps/web/src/lib/chunk-text.test.ts`) covers at minimum: empty/whitespace input, single-chunk (under limit), multi-sentence packing at boundaries, an over-long single sentence hard-split, the no-chunk-exceeds-maxLength invariant, and the content-preservation invariant.
`npm run build:web` and `npm run test` both pass green.
outOfScope: Wiring `chunkText` into the synthesis dispatch path or any caller (this is the standalone utility + tests only).
Looking up provider-specific `maxTextLength` values (caller passes `maxLength` directly).
Language-specific sentence segmentation beyond basic `.`, `!`, `?` punctuation boundaries.
Any stateful behavior, side effects, or I/O — the function is pure.
problemStatement: Callers who need to send text to synthesis providers have no safe, reusable way to split input that exceeds a provider's maximum text length. Without this, long-form content is either silently truncated (losing content) or rejected by the provider, leaving users with no path to synthesize extended text.
userValue: A well-tested, sentence-aware chunker gives any caller a reliable building block to break long text into provider-sized pieces without cutting mid-word, dropping content, or scrambling order. This unlocks long-form synthesis across the platform and eliminates ad-hoc, error-prone truncation that today loses user content.
